title: websocket.py: no validation of subscription event names
description:
File: python/src/lichen/sim/websocket.py
Lines: 409-415

handle_websocket 'subscribe' command does not validate that event names are strings:

    events = data.get('events', [])
    if isinstance(events, list):
        client.subscriptions.update(events)

A client could send {"cmd": "subscribe", "events": [1, null, {}]} and add non-string items to the subscription set. Since is_subscribed() checks 'event_type in self.subscriptions' where event_type is always a string, the subscription would silently fail to match anything.

Fix: Validate that all items in events list are strings before adding.

Impact: Minor - malicious clients can break their own subscriptions; no security impact.
notes: Fixed in commit f76a1e1 (validate subscription events are strings). No further changes needed.
close_reason: Validation for string event names in subscribe/unsubscribe already implemented in commit f76a1e198
